title: Connection<T...>​
content: </> interface Connection<T...> { Connected: boolean Disconnect: (Connection<T...>) → () Reconnect: (Connection<T...>) → () }

title: Disconnect​
content: </> Connection.Disconnect(self: Connection<T...>) → () Disconnects the connection from the signal. May be reconnected later using Connection:Reconnect(). local signal = Signal.new() local connection = signal:Connect(print, 'TEST1:') signal:Fire 'Hello world!' -- 'TEST1:	Hello world!' connection:Disconnect() signal:Fire 'Goodbye world!'

title: Reconnect​
content: </> Connection.Reconnect(self: Connection<T...>) → () Reconnects the connection to the signal again. May be disconnected later using Connection:Disconnect(). local signal = Signal.new() local connection = signal:Connect(print, 'TEST1:') signal:Fire 'Hello world!' -- 'TEST1:	Hello world!' connection:Disconnect() signal:Fire 'Goodbye world!' connection:Reconnect() signal:Fire 'Hello again!' -- 'TEST1:	Hello again!'

title: Signal<U...>​
content: </> interface Signal<U...> { Connect: ( Signal<U...>, fn: (...any) → (), T... ) → Connection<T...> Once: ( Signal<U...>, fn: (...any) → (), T... ) → Connection<T...> Fire: ( Signal<U...>, U... ) → () DisconnectAll: (Signal<U...>) → () }

title: new​
content: </> Signal.new() → Signal Returns a new signal instance which can be used to connect functions. local signal = Signal.new()

title: Connect​
content: </> Signal.Connect( self: Signal<U...>, fn: (...any) → (), ...: T... ) → Connection Connects a function to the signal and returns the connection. Passes variadic arguments that will always be prepended to fired arguments. local signal = Signal.new() local connection = signal:Connect(print, 'TEST1:') signal:Fire 'Hello world!' -- 'TEST1:	Hello world!'

title: DisconnectAll​
content: </> Signal.DisconnectAll(self: Signal<U...>) → () Disconnects all connections currently connected to the signal. They may be reconnected later. local signal = Signal.new() local connection = signal:Connect(print, 'TEST1:') signal:Fire 'Hello world!' -- 'TEST1:	Hello world!' signal:DisconnectAll() signal:Fire 'Goodbye World!'

title: Fire​
content: </> Signal.Fire( self: Signal<U...>, ...: U... ) → () Fires the signal and calls all connected functions with the connection's bound arguments and the fired arguments. local signal = Signal.new() local connection = signal:Connect(print, 'TEST1:') signal:Fire 'Hello world!' -- 'TEST1:	Hello world!' signal:DisconnectAll() signal:Fire 'Goodbye World!'

title: Wait​
content: </> Signal.Wait(self: Signal<U...>) → U... Waits until the signal is fired and returns the fired arguments. local signal = Signal.new() local connection = signal:Connect(print, 'TEST1:') signal:Fire 'Hello world!' -- 'TEST1:	Hello world!' signal:DisconnectAll() signal:Fire 'Goodbye World!'

title: Once​
content: </> Signal.Once( self: Signal<U...>, fn: (...any) → (), ...: T... ) → () Connects a function to a signal and disconnects if after the first fire. Can be reconnected later. local signal = Signal.new() local connection = signal:Once(print, 'TEST1:') signal:Fire 'Hello world!' -- 'TEST1:	Hello world!' signal:Fire 'Goodbye World!'
